commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=332f3ef2016cfa82320bf9223f9e0af516df50c2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `routeReply` fallback for compaction notices when `agents.defaults.compaction.notifyUser` is enabled and no block-reply callback is available.

PR surface: Source +51, Tests +78. Total +129 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main returns from `sendCompactionNotice` when `onBlockReply` is absent, while docs say `notifyUser` sends notices. The queued follow-up path is also visible in source and does not apply the same notice contract.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer follow-up because the externally authored PR is mock-only proof and the fix shape must cover the queued/proactive path before merge.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): route compaction notices without block replies
target.body:
## Summary
- Route `agents.defaults.compaction.notifyUser` notices through the originating channel when an active block-reply callback is unavailable.
- Preserve the existing `onBlockReply` path for live streaming turns.
- Keep notice delivery non-fatal if there is no routable origin or the fallback send fails.

## Linked context
Closes #88933

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: compaction notices with `notifyUser: true` were silently dropped when a compaction event ran without `opts.onBlockReply`.
- Real environment tested: Linux contributor checkout; focused OpenClaw auto-reply tests.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- Evidence after fix: targeted regression passes and asserts the no-`onBlockReply` path calls `routeReply` with the compaction notice, origin channel, destination, `replyKind: "block"`, and `mirror: false`.
- Observed result after fix: `agent-runner-execution.test.ts` passed 153 tests.
- What was not tested: a live WhatsApp/Telegram/Discord compaction delivery.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this contributor environment cannot safely trigger a real user-channel compaction notice without using private local channel/account state.
- Before evidence: current source returned early from `sendCompactionNotice` whenever `params.opts?.onBlockReply` was absent.

## Tests and validation
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `git diff --check -- src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`

## Risk checklist
Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`
Highest-risk area: fallback delivery sends an additional notice through the origin route when block streaming is unavailable. The mitigation is to require a routable origin, avoid transcript mirroring for the notice, keep failures non-fatal, and preserve the old callback path when present.

## Current review state
Ready for maintainer review. Waiting on CI and any maintainer-requested live channel proof.
target.author: charles-openclaw
